notes.note: Decimus Brutus is addressed with his titles: IMP. (imperator, the soldiers' acclamation won by his defense of Mutina) and COS. DESIG. (consul-designate, named for 42 BC). The salutation formula S. D. is salutem dicit, "sends greetings."

notes.anchor: as before you freed the republic from a king, so now you will free it from kingship
notes.note: A pointed play on rex (king) and regnum (kingship): the first "king" is the dictator Caesar, whom Decimus helped assassinate; the "kingship" now to be overthrown is Antony's bid for autocratic power.

notes.anchor: A levy is being held at Rome and throughout all Italy
notes.note: The dilectus was the conscription of troops; Cicero stresses that in the crisis of 43 BC men were volunteering rather than waiting to be enrolled.
